activities_projects.title: Lithospheric Plate Movement Simulation
activities_projects.objective: Understand how convection currents in the mantle move plates
steps: Fill the beaker with water and place small pieces of paper on the surface
Heat the water from the bottom and observe the path of the paper pieces
Place two small cardboard pieces (plates) on the water surface
Observe how they move apart or collide as the water (magma) circulates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Explains the relationship between internal heat and tectonic plate movement
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct description of the circular motion of the paper/cardboard (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Mapping the 'Ring of Fire'
activities_projects.objective: Identify the relationship between plate boundaries and volcanoes
steps: Plot the locations of major active volcanoes (e.g., Mt. Fuji, Mt. Etna) on the map
Draw the major tectonic plate boundaries using a dashed line
Observe the concentration of volcanoes along the edges of the Pacific Plate
Label this region as the 'Circum-Pacific Belt' or 'Ring of Fire'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes the distribution of endogenic forces along plate margins
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the volcano plotting and boundary alignment (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Seismic Wave Intensity Chart
activities_projects.objective: Understand the Richter scale and the spread of earthquake energy
steps: Shine a flashlight on a wall from a distance of 1 foot and measure the circle of light
Move back to 5 feet and observe how the light spreads but gets dimmer
Relate the 'Focus' to the flashlight and the 'Epicenter' to the wall
Create a table showing the destructive capacity of Richter scale levels 2.0, 5.0, and 7.0
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Explains how the intensity of an earthquake decreases with distance from the epicenter
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct interpretation of the Richter scale data (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Ox-bow Lake Evolution Model
activities_projects.objective: Trace the transformation of a river meander into a lake
steps: Create a winding channel (meander) in the sand tray
Pour water slowly and observe erosion on the outer curve and deposition on the inner curve
Simulate a 'flood' by increasing water flow until the meander neck is cut off
Identify the isolated water body as an 'Ox-bow lake'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Demonstrates the dual processes of erosion and deposition in river landforms
activities_projects.evaluation: Scientific explanation of the 'cutoff' process (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Mushroom Rock: Wind Abrasion Study
activities_projects.objective: Analyze why wind erosion is more effective on lower rock surfaces
steps: Rub the bottom half of a chalk column vigorously with sandpaper for 2 minutes
Rub the top half gently for only 30 seconds
Observe the resulting 'mushroom' shape with a narrow base and wide top
Explain how wind carries heavier sand particles closer to the ground
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects the height of wind-blown particles to the specific shape of desert landforms

activities_projects.title: Coastal Erosion Sequence
activities_projects.objective: Understand the progressive formation of Sea Arches and Stacks
steps: Shape a clay cliff and blow air/water at a small point to create a 'Sea Cave'
Continue until the hole goes through to form a 'Sea Arch'
Simulate the roof collapsing to leave a vertical pillar called a 'Stack'
Label each stage in your observation log
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes the transition of landforms over time due to wave action
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of the stages of coastal erosion (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Glacial Moraine Sorting
activities_projects.objective: Differentiate between glacial deposits and river deposits
materials: Ice block mixed with sand and pebbles
Glass of water with sand and pebbles
steps: Stir the glass of water and observe how materials settle by size (sorted)
Let the ice block melt on a flat surface and observe the unsorted pile (Moraine)
Compare the 'sorting' ability of water vs. the 'dumping' nature of ice
Write a note on why glacial moraines are unsorted
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Distinguishes the characteristics of glacial deposition
activities_projects.evaluation: Comparative analysis of sorted vs. unsorted sediments (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Floodplain Siltation Test
activities_projects.objective: Observe how levees and floodplains are formed
steps: Pour muddy water down the center of a slanted board
Observe how the heavy pebbles stay near the 'river bank' (Levee)
Observe how fine silt spreads over the flat area (Floodplain)
Explain why floodplains are fertile
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the depositional logic behind river valley landforms
activities_projects.evaluation: Diagram showing the arrangement of sediments after the 'flood' (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Endogenic vs. Exogenic Force Sorting
activities_projects.objective: Categorize Earth's movements based on their energy source
materials: Cards with landform names (e.g., Delta, Waterfall, Volcano, Mountain range)
steps: Classify 'Sudden Movements' and 'Diastrophic Forces' under Endogenic
Classify 'Erosional and Depositional' activities under Exogenic
Identify which force creates new land and which wears it down
Summarize the relationship between these two opposing forces
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Synthesizes the internal and external drivers of landscape change
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy in classification and conceptual clarity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Local Disaster Preparedness Plan
activities_projects.objective: Apply earthquake safety knowledge to a specific environment
materials: Map of your home or school
steps: Identify 'Safe Spots' (under sturdy furniture) with green stickers
Identify 'Danger Spots' (near windows, mirrors, or heavy cabinets) with red stickers
Plan an evacuation route that leads to an open area
Draft a 3-step 'Drop, Cover, Hold on' instruction sheet
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops practical safety skills based on scientific understanding of seismic waves
activities_projects.evaluation: Feasibility and completeness of the safety plan (10 marks)
